message:
br-bd-dc9y5 implement mknod for char/block devices, FIFOs, sockets

Pre-fix dispatch_mknod rejected every non-regular file type with
EOPNOTSUPP, blocking overlayfs-as-upper whiteouts (S_IFCHR with
rdev = makedev(0, 0)), POSIX FIFOs, Unix-domain socket files, and
generic /dev-style device nodes through the FUSE mount.

FsOps::mknod trait method (default returns ENOTSUP):
- New trait fn rounding out FsOps's create surface alongside create
  (regular files), mkdir, and symlink. ext4 backend overrides; btrfs
  returns ENOTSUP until a separate btrfs path lands.

OpenFs::ext4_mknod:
- Validates the S_IFMT bits and routes the file-type to the matching
  Ext4FileType for the directory entry: S_IFCHR -> Chrdev, S_IFBLK ->
  Blkdev, S_IFIFO -> Fifo, S_IFSOCK -> Sock. Anything else (including
  S_IFREG, which belongs in `create`) returns EINVAL.
- FIFO/socket reject non-zero rdev with EINVAL — POSIX mknod(2)
  semantics.
- Allocates the inode via ffs_inode::create_inode with the full mode
  (no S_IFREG OR), then post-processes for non-regular semantics:
  clears EXT4_EXTENTS_FL, zeroes inode.size + inode.blocks (devices
  own no data), and for char/block writes rdev into the i_block area
  using the old 16-bit format when it fits (i_block[0..4]) or the
  new 32-bit format otherwise (i_block[4..8]) — matching Linux's
  ext4_encode_dev preference.
- Two rollback paths: write_inode failure -> delete_inode rollback;
  ext4_add_dir_entry failure -> delete_inode rollback.

dispatch_mknod (ffs-fuse) now branches:
- Regular file with rdev=0 -> ops.create() (legacy fast path,
  identical bytes-on-disk to before).
- S_IFCHR/S_IFBLK/S_IFIFO/S_IFSOCK -> ops.mknod(full_mode, rdev, …).
- Everything else -> EOPNOTSUPP.

OpenFs::mknod public convenience method mirrors `create` /
`mkdir`'s shape so in-process callers can exercise the path.

Tests: 672 ffs-core --lib + 250 ffs-fuse --lib pass. Five new
ffs-core regressions:
- mknod_whiteout_char_device: rdev=0 char device round-trips with
  kind=CharDevice, rdev=0, size=blocks=0.
- mknod_block_device_preserves_rdev: 16-bit rdev for /dev/sda1
  (makedev(8,1)=0x0801) round-trips through getattr.
- mknod_fifo_and_socket_succeed_with_zero_rdev: both file types
  create + return the right FileType.
- mknod_fifo_with_nonzero_rdev_returns_einval: FIFO + rdev != 0
  surfaces EINVAL.
- mknod_unsupported_file_type_returns_einval: S_IFREG via mknod
  (wrong-API misuse) is rejected rather than silently routed.

fmt + clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_dc9y5.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -669,6 +669,34 @@ pub trait FsOps: Send + Sync {
         Err(FfsError::ReadOnly)
     }
 
+    /// `mknod(2)` for non-regular file types.
+    ///
+    /// `mode` carries the full S_IF* + permission bits. `rdev` is the
+    /// `makedev(2)`-encoded device number, ignored for FIFO and socket.
+    /// Regular-file creation goes through [`Self::create`] and dirs
+    /// through [`Self::mkdir`]; this method handles char/block devices,
+    /// FIFOs, and sockets — the file types that overlayfs whiteouts,
+    /// POSIX named pipes, and Unix-domain socket files depend on.
+    ///
+    /// The default implementation returns `ENOTSUP` so backends that
+    /// only support regular files (e.g. tests, read-only mounts) are
+    /// not silently broken when callers expect device-creation
+    /// semantics.
+    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
+    fn mknod(
+        &self,
+        _cx: &Cx,
+        _scope: &mut RequestScope,
+        _parent: InodeNumber,
+        _name: &OsStr,
+        _mode: u16,
+        _rdev: u32,
+        _uid: u32,
+        _gid: u32,
+    ) -> ffs_error::Result<InodeAttr> {
+        Err(FfsError::Io(std::io::Error::from_raw_os_error(libc::ENOTSUP)))
+    }
+
     /// Rename with `renameat2(2)` flags (FUSE_RENAME2 opcode).
     ///
     /// `flags` is the bitset defined by `<linux/fs.h>`:
